seoTitle: Charleys Forest vs Charlotte Bay — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Charleys Forest and Charlotte Bay. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Charleys Forest vs Charlotte Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Charleys Forest and Charlotte Bay.
heading: Charleys Forest vs Charlotte Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Charleys Forest, New South Wales and Charlotte Bay, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Charleys Forest has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Charlotte Bay (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Charleys Forest or Charlotte Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Charleys Forest has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Charlotte Bay scores 23/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Charleys Forest scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. Charlotte Bay scores 57/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Charleys Forest or Charlotte Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Charleys Forest has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
